1137  {XII).  Bareme  des  quotee·parte  pour  la repartition  dee  depeneee  de  l'Organiaation dee  Nations  Uniee
text_en: 1137 {XII). Share scale for the distribution of the Organization of the United Nations

text_fr: L'Assemblee generale,

Rappelanl ses  resolutions  14  (I)  du  13  fevrier  1946, 238  (III)  du  18  novembre  1948  et  665  (VII)  du 5  decembre  1952,  concernant  la  repartition,  entre  les Etats  Membres,  des  depenses  de  !'Organisation  des Nations Unies et la fixation  du pourcentage que ne doit depasser  la quote-part  d'aucun Etat  Membre,
text_en: Recalling its resolutions 14 (I) of 13 February 1946, 238 (III) of 18 November 1948 and 665 (VII) of 5 December 1952 concerning the distribution among Member States of the expenditure of the United Nations and the determination of the percentage which must not exceed the share of any Member State,

text_fr: N otant que,  lorsque ce plafond a ete  fixe a 33,33 pour 100 a compter  du  ler  janvier  1954,  !'Organisation comprenait  soixante  Etats  Membres,
text_en: However, when this ceiling was fixed at 33.33 per cent from 1 January 1954, the Organization included sixty Member States,

text_fr: Notant  en  outre que,  depuis  le  ler  Janvier  1954, vingt-deux Etats ont ete admis a l'Orgamsation,
text_en: Noting further that, since 1 January 1954, twenty-two States have been admitted to the Orgamsation,

text_fr: -Rappelant sa resolution  1087  (XI)  du  21  decembre 1956,  en  vertu  de  laquelle  les  quotes-parts  des  seize
text_en: -Recalling its resolution 1087 (XI) of 21 December 1956, according to which the shares of the

text_fr: so

premiers  Etats  admis a !'Organisation  depuis  le  ler janvier  1954  ont  ete  incluses  dans  le  bareme  regulier des  contributions  pour  1956  et  1957  et  ont  servi a reduire  la  quote-part  de  tous  les  Etats  Membres, a !'exception  de  celui  qui  verse  la  contribution  la  plus elevee et de ceux  qui  versent la contribution minimum,
text_en: the first States admitted to the Organization since 1 January 1954 have been included in the regular scale of contributions for 1956 and 1957 and have served to reduce the quota of all Member States, with the exception of the one who pays the highest contribution and those who pay the minimum contribution,

text_fr: N otant qu'il  y  a  maintenant  six  nouveaux  Etats Membres la  Federation  de  Malaisie,  le  Ghana,  le J apon,  le  Maroc,  le  Soudan  et la  Tunisie dont  la quote-part  n'a encore  ete  ni  fixee  par  le  Comite  des contributions  ni incluse  dans  les  100  pour  100 du bareme  des  quotes-parts,
text_en: With the exception of six new Member States, the Federation of Malaysia, Ghana, Japan, Morocco, Sudan and Tunisia, whose assessments have not yet been fixed by the Committee or included in the 100 per cent of the scale of assessments,

text_fr: Decide ce  qui  suit:

items_fr: 1. En principe,  la contribution maximum d'un Etat Membre aux depenses  ordinaires de l'Organisation des Nations  Unies  ne  doit  pas  depasser  30  pour  100  du total;

2. Les contributions correspondant aux quotes-parts fixees  par  le  Comite  des  contributions  pour  1956  et 1957  en ce  qui  concerne le  Japon, le Maroc,  le  Soudan et  la Tunisie,  et pour  1957  en  ce  qui  concerne la  Fede› ration  de  Malaisie  et  le Ghana,  seront  considerees comme  recettes  accessoires  de  !'Organisation;
3. Lorsqu'il preparera  le  bareme  des  quotes-parts pour  1958  et  pour· 1es  exercices  ulterieurs,  le  Comite des  contributions  agira  comme  suit:

a) Les  quotes-parts  fixees  par le  Comite  des  contri› butions pour  1958 en  ce  qui  concerne la  Federation de Malaisie,  le  Ghana,  le  Japon,  le  Maroc,  le  Soudan  et la  Tunisie  seront  incluses  dans  les  100  pour  100  du bareme  de  1958;  pour  cette  operation,  on  utilisera  la somme  des  quotes-parts  des  six  Etats  Membres  pre› cites  afin  de  reduire  proportionnellement  les  quotes› parts  de  tous  les  E.tats  Membresa !'exception  de ceux  qui  versent  la  quote-part  minimum en  tenant compte  du  principe  du  maximum  par  habitant  et  de toute  reduction  qui  pourrait  devenir  necessaire  une fois  que  le  Comite  des  contributions  aura  examine, a sa  session  commen<;ant  le  15  octobre  1957,  les  recours relatifs a des recommandations  anteriaires dudit . comite;

b) Au cours de la periode de trois ans correspondant au  prochain  bareme  des  quotes-parts  (1959-1961),  le Comite  des  contributions  recommandera  de  nouvelles mesures  en  vue  de  reduire  la  quote-part  de  l'Etat Membre qui verse la contribution la plus elevee,  lorsque de  nouveaux  Etats  seront  admis a l'Organisation des Nations  Unies;
c) Par la  suite,  le  Comite  des  contributions  recom› mandera  !'adoption  des  mesures  supplementaires  qui pourront  etre  necessaires  et  appropriees  pour  achever la reduction ;
d) Les  quotes-parts  des  Etats  Membres  ne  seront en aucun cas majorees du fait  de la presente resolution.
items_en: 1. In principle, the maximum contribution of a Member State to the regular expenses of the United Nations shall not exceed 30 per cent of the total;

2. Contributions to the assessed contributions fixed by the Committee for 1956 and 1957 in respect of Japan, Morocco, Sudan and Tunisia, and for 1957 in respect of the Fede›ration of Malaysia and Ghana, shall be considered as miscellaneous income of the Organization;
3. When preparing the scale of assessments for 1958 and for the first subsequent financial years, the Committee on Contributions shall act as follows:

(a) The contributions fixed by the Committee on Contributions for 1958 in respect of the Federation of Malaysia, Ghana, Japan, Morocco, Sudan and Tunisia will be included in the 100 per cent of the 1958 scale; for this operation, the sum of the assessed contributions of the six Member States mentioned above will be used in order to reduce proportionately the shares of all Member Statesa except those which pay the minimum assessment, taking into account the principle of the maximum per capita share and any reduction which may become necessary once the Committee on Contributions has considered, at its session commencing on 15 October 1957, the appeals relating to prior recommendations of the said Committee;

(b) During the three-year period corresponding to the next scale of assessments (1959-1961), the Committee on Contributions will recommend further measures to reduce the quota of the Member State paying the highest contribution, when new States are admitted to the United Nations;
(c) Subsequently, the Committee on Contributions will recommend the adoption of additional measures which may be necessary and appropriate to complete the reduction;
(d) The assessments of Member States will in no way be increased as a result of the present resolution.
